name: Sour Cream Raisin Pie
recipeIngredient: 3/4 tablespoon spices (combination of cinnamon, nutmeg, cloves, and allspice)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
2. In a food processor, pulse raisins with sugar until finely chopped. Avoid overprocessing or processing just the raisins without the sugar; otherwise, it will turn to goo.
3. Beat eggs and gently fold in sour cream. Fold in the sugar, raisin mixture, and spices. Cook on the stove until thick, using low heat. This can burn easily if not monitored.
4. Pour the mixture into the 9-inch pie shell and cover with the top crust. Cut some vent holes and sprinkle with sugar. Cover the edges of the crust with foil strips, shiny side up (or your favorite crust protector). Remove the strips for the last 10-15 minutes to allow the crust to brown.
5. Bake at 375°F for about 1 hour or until the crust is browned.
Serving suggestion: Serve with a dollop of hard sauce.
